new_classes.label_en: Grape Must in Fermentation or with Fermentation Arrested Otherwise than by Addition of Alcohol (CN 2204 30 10)
new_classes.label_de: Traubenmost, teilweise gegoren, auch ohne Alkohol stummgemacht (KN 2204 30 10)
new_classes.definition_en: Grape must that is either still in the process of fermentation or whose fermentation has been arrested by means other than the addition of alcohol, classified under CN 2204 30 10. This covers partially fermented grape musts where fermentation arrest is achieved by non-alcoholic means (e.g. cooling, sulphitation, sterile filtration) rather than by adding ethyl alcohol.

new_classes.label_en: Fermentation Arrested Without Alcohol Addition Quality
new_classes.definition_en: A quality borne by a grape must whose fermentation has been arrested by means other than the addition of alcohol (e.g. by cooling, sulphur dioxide treatment, or sterile filtration), distinguishing CN 2204 30 10 from CN 2204 30 92/96 where alcohol is added to arrest fermentation.

A quality borne by a grape must that is currently undergoing active fermentation, i.e. where the conversion of sugars to alcohol and carbon dioxide by yeast is still in progress. This quality discriminates CN 2204 30 10 (in fermentation) from other grape must subheadings.

coverage_explanation: The two primary distinguishing criteria of CN 2204 30 10 are captured: (1) grape must in active fermentation (InFermentationQuality) and (2) grape must with fermentation arrested by non-alcoholic means (FermentationArrestedWithoutAlcoholQuality). The class is correctly placed under GrapeMust2204. However, the legal text references additional notes 2(a), 2(b), 2(c) and 3 to Chapter 22, which detail specific density/sugar thresholds and actual alcoholic strength boundaries that further delimit the subheading. These numeric thresholds (e.g. density, actual vs. potential alcohol) cannot be fully expressed without introducing additional datatype properties and data ranges for those note-specific criteria, which are not yet in the TBox. The disjunction between the two qualifying conditions (in fermentation OR arrested without alcohol) is approximated by providing two separate someValuesFrom restrictions on the same class, which models the OR semantics via class membership in either condition — a slight over-approximation compared to a strict OWL union. Coverage is reduced accordingly to 0.80.
